Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The University of Texas at Dallas branch of 180 Degrees Consulting was founded in Fall 2018. We began as a handful of students wanting to make an impact in our local community. Now, we are an organization of dozens of students working with leading non-profits and social ventures at the local, national, and international level. We aim to revolutionize and bring change to ultimately make the world a better place.

Our covers are filmed and uploaded onto our YouTube channel. 200% also have the opportunity to not only collaborate with other Kpop dance groups but also perform at live events such as the annual Korean Festival and the Mid-Autumn Festival. Our mission is to provide a safe, comfortable community of shared interest and cultural diversity of UT Dallas and its students.

Title: Academics for Learning
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The objectives of the Academics for Learning club are to provide a group for students to read, discuss, and further their knowledge in rigorous subjects and fields. Members will gain valuable understanding of various educational and professional topics, and can record their accomplishments in the club.

Title: Accessible Prosthetics Initiative UT Dallas Chapter
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our mission is to increase access to prosthetic technologies and care. We emphasize serving populations who do not have access to care through other avenues. API tackles this goal in a holistic manner through community outreach, accessible device design, and educational initiatives.

Title: Accounting Leadership Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Accounting Leadership Association (ALA) at The University of Texas at Dallas is a student-led, professional and academic organization founded to provide the accounting academic community of the Naveen Jindal School of Management (JSOM) professional, academic, and networking opportunities.

Title: Accounting Leadership Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: ALA's mission is to support the UT Dallas accounting community. by creating networking opportunities, developing professional skills, and educating students about the various accounting career paths.

Title: Active Minds at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Active Minds aims to change the conversation surrounding mental health at the University of Texas at Dallas, increasing awareness among students, faculty, and staff regarding mental health issues and resources and reducing stigma associated with mental illness. Active Minds encourages open discussion and serves as a liaison between our campus and the mental health community by holding campus-wide events and organizing fundraisers and holistic health initiatives. Active Minds members facilitate the goals of Active Minds by serving as proactive mental health advocates, volunteering at events and growing a safe environment for mental health conversations.

Title: Actuarial Student Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The ASA is an educational organization dedicated to students interested in pursuing a career in actuarial science with the purpose of preparing students for their future career by supporting exam progression and promoting professional development through leadership opportunities, mentorship, and networking.

Title: Advancements in Modern Medicine
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
AMED’s mission is to enlighten college students about modern medical advancements by providing leadership and support for those on the pre-health journey.

AMED is an educational and academic organization at UTD with the purpose to explore the future of technological and pharmaceutical advancements in medicine. Giving insight to pre-health students about new innovations and inventions in the medical field, this organization aims to look into the future at how the medical field is changing as technology advances. By being a pre-health organization, we aim to provide a strong support system for understanding the rigorous lives of college students and future healthcare workers. Our support group allows for a diverse community of colleagues emphasizing work-life balance and mental health awareness.

The club is effectively run by and for its members, giving all parties the spotlight to shed their expertise and research with others. This will allow them to express their passion for medicine as well as understand the future of medicine. We strive to have an open environment for all who wish to participate in our club

Not only that, but when the time comes we are alumni, we will continue to be involved within the organization helping the underclassmen as well as staying involved to the school that gave us so much. Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Advocates for Sexual Health values the sexual health of all individuals and understands that sexual health includes emotional, psychological, and physical dimensions. AFP collegiate chapter will

•	Prepare students for their future career through mentoring, leadership opportunities, and a wide AFP network.

The UTD AFP Collegiate Chapter is also part of something much bigger - a worldwide professional organization that not only is a part of a global network of fundraising professionals across the globe, but also a catalyst for an amazing career.

Title: Agaram Tamil Student Organization

2) To serve as a forum that brings together Tamil students, students affiliated with the Tamil language, and people interested in Tamil together through various social activities, volunteering opportunities, and traditional events.

3) To focus on providing services such as food drives, workshops, and fundraisers while being educational in terms of promoting the culture’s language and arts.

4) To organize events that are welcoming to all, as opportunities to bring together Tamil people.

5) To maintain a respectful and friendly community that Tamil students can feel a part of within The University of Texas at Dallas.

6) To help fellow Tamil students and create an inspiring image to encourage younger Tamil students towards their future endeavors.

Title: Ahmadiyya Muslim Students Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Description: To further the message of the International Ahmadiyya Muslim Community, through activities and events, based on the motto, “Love for all, hatred for none”.

The mission statement of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Students Association (AMSA) is to spread awareness and general appreciation of Islam and its true doctrines to all Muslims and non-Muslims alike, as well as humanitarian services to all regardless of creed, color, race, or religion. The AMSA shall also work to spread peace, tolerance, and understanding, in the UTD community. 

The purpose of the AMSA is as follows: To provide a better understanding of Islam and clarifying misconceptions surrounding it, in conjunction with promoting peaceful co-existence with all individuals regardless of race, creed, or religion and providing humanitarian service to better serve the university and local community; Organize events benefitting the students, faculty and greater community including but not limited to blood drives, charitable fund-raising and disaster assistance; Organize events approved and initiated by the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community and/or Ahmadiyya Muslim Youth Association, governing executive body of this local chapter of AMSA, such as interfaith seminars to promote religious harmony, tolerance and respect for all students, faculty, and community members regardless of their beliefs 

The Ahmadiyya Muslim Youth Association’s (MKA USA) Student Affairs Department and the National AMSA Board is the governing body of this chapter of AMSA, and shall institute the following as to the purpose of this organization: Create an awareness of Islam through service and programming; Create an awareness of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community’s motto: Love for all, Hatred for None; Encourage students to participate in Islamic events to gain a better understanding; Be the official “go to” organization for questions/concerns/issues regarding Islam; Strive to spread the message of peace, reconciliation, and fraternity across communities.

Title: Airsoft Club

The club will enhance members' communication effectiveness, situational awareness, and technical skills. This club accepts any and all students, with no level of fitness or experience required.

Through this club, we hope to raise money and help extend Akshaya Patra??s positive influence to even more kids.

AED offers opportunities for intellectual and professional development, provides a forum for students with common interests, and extends a program of service to benefit the college/university community. Alpha Epsilon Delta is affiliated with the American Association for the Advancement of Science and is a member of the Association of College Honor Societies.

Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated ®, an international service organization, was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek-letter organization established by African American college-educated women.

MISSION STATEMENT
The sisterhood of alpha Kappa Delta Phi provides women with a sense of belonging that nurtures life-long friendships, develops self-empowered leaders, and strengthens communities through service, scholarship, and Asian-Awareness.

VISION STATEMENT

aKDPhi envisions a worldwide network of respected, confident women who elevate each other and their communities through compassionate, purpose-driven leadership that ensures diversity, encourages innovation, and promotes collaboration.

Title: AMERICAN INSTITUTE OF AERONAUTICS AND ASTRONAUTICS UTD

Title: American Institute of Graphic Arts at UTD (AIGA UTD)
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: We are one of the largest design organizations here at UTD. We welcome both design and non-design students. Just come with an interest and passion for design.

Title: American Marketing Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The American Marketing Association strives to be the most relevant force and voice shaping marketing around the world, an essential community for marketers.

In 2015, the “Next AMA” launched four transformational strategies for its North American operations:

Committing to Service Leadership to customers, members and stakeholders.
Providing a proactive, differentiated and modern voice for a single unified, One AMA brand, One AMA Community.
Establishing an Intellectual Agenda that is harmonized across all platforms and channels based on an editorial strategy to feature the tension between Best Versus Next Practices™.
Building a personalized and connected Marketplace of knowledge that engages the AMA in a single community—always on, always connected … One AMA Community.

Title: American Medical Student Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: We are a pre-health organization which aims to foster relationships between aspiring medical students and healthcare professionals. Our primary focus is providing club members with access to volunteering, shadowing, and research opportunities, in order to fulfill our mission & create a community of socialized and well-rounded future physicians with experience in all aspects of healthcare. Members attend monthly meetings which feature doctors & researchers as guest speakers, along with components designed to promote the practice of ethical decision making and knowledge in current events.

Title: American Medical Women's Association at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The American Medical Women's Association is an established national organization dedicated towards advancing opportunities for women in medicine, advocate for equity, and ensure excellence in healthcare. Our chapter at the University of Texas at Dallas is dedicated towards fostering a healthier world where women in healthcare achieve equity in the medical profession and realize their full potential. 

	Throughout the semester, we will provide various volunteer opportunities, organize fundraisers and health clinics for women in underserved areas, host guest speakers, and have social and professional conferences online and across the country with other AMWA chapters at the pre-med, medical school, and professional level (Pre-Medical Sessions including resume/CV reviews, mock interviews, hands-on workshops, and more). We at AMWA UTD welcome individuals from various different pre-health backgrounds (pre-med, pre-PA, pre-dental, pre-pharmacy, and more!).

Title: American Muslim Women Physician Association at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The American Muslim Women Physician Association is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that empowers women by improving their health and welfare and brings Muslim women doctors, dentists and healthcare professionals in communication with each other for encouragement and camaraderie. The mission of the American Muslim Women Physicians Association at UT Dallas is to improve the condition of women’s health, provide a platform for Muslim women in medicine, and raise awareness of issues regarding community welfare. The UTD chapter aims to promote networking and leadership among students pursuing a career in medicine through research opportunities and preventive health campaigns.

Title: American Physician Scientist Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The University of Texas at Dallas APSA Chapter has been established for the purpose of educating, supporting, and mentoring individuals considering and actively pursuing a future career as a physician-scientist.

Title: American Red Cross Club at The University of Texas at Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Red Cross UTD is dedicated to promoting awareness of the humanitarian goals of the American Red Cross by participating as a student chapter in the DFW Metroplex. We aim to contribute to the prevention and alleviation of suffering in the face of disaster while upholding the American Red Cross Code of Conduct and the seven Fundamental Principles of the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies.

Title: American Sign Language organization
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: To educate others on Deaf culture and provide a safe space for individuals to learn and practice American Sign Language

Title: American Society of Mechanical Engineers

We pride ourselves on creating a unique, exciting environment that embraces all types of students and help promotes forming connections between UTD engineering students and preparing them for their future careers.

Title: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our mission is to connect the mechanical engineering majors at utdallas and facilitate a learning environment. We host social events, workshops, speaker events, and we have weekly competition meetings.

Title: Analytics Memory In Database
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: AIMDB was started in fall'19 with the aim to create awareness and develop a better understanding of the nature of analytics on In-Memory Database. The club mainly focuses on the technologies such as SAP HANA, and Salesforce. The club was constituted for providing understanding in In-memory Database Mechanism, its hardware requirements and configuration, and to study equipment and processes related to big data and analytics. We offer various events some of which include Industry field trips to observe real world use of AIMDB and Analytics, utilizing state of art technology and methods in-practice on IMDB

Title: Anim3
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
We at A3 are the official anime club of UT Dallas. We welcome all UTD students, staff, alumni, or acquaintances of the aforementioned groups. Although anyone can watch anime in the comfort of their own homes, watching with others is also a great way to enjoy shows! We at A3 feel this sense of camaraderie is one of our greatest strengths. Everyone tends to have a wide array of tastes, yet we all come together to enjoy the same shows. Aside from shows, our other activities include Kahoot trivia nights, Iron Cosplay, local events, anime merch swap/trade meets, potlucks, etc. We’re always open to new ideas for meetings as well so feel free to participate in the discussion.
Now you may be wondering, why A3? The 3 stands for our three tenets: culture, social, and education. We seek to do what other anime clubs don't. We explore the culture in anime and integrate it into meetings and host grounds for making friends and establishing bonds. Not only can we take the time to appreciate anime and manga for what it is, but we can also take the time to dissect them and see how to better the lives of others as well.

Title: Animation Guild
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Animation Guild's mission centers around building a community of aspiring animation professionals. The Guild seeks to foster more collaboration among students and build stronger relationship with animation studios, alumni, faculty, and the local community.

Title: Anime Orchestra Ensemble
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
AOE is a student led orchestra which focuses on anime and video game music. 
Our mission is:  “To give people the opportunity to perform non-traditional orchestral music primarily sourced from anime and video games.” and “To provide a vibrant community within which fans of various series can meet, connect, and further explore their favorite genres.”

Title: Arab Student Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our organization aims to unite and serve the Arab community on campus while educating the UTD student body about the Arab culture. We will serve and accommodate to all students, Arab and non-Arab alike, promote Arab culture and heritage, adding diversity to the UTD community.

Title: Armenian Student Association at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: ASA shall be a cultural organization created to promote appreciation of Armenian culture and heritage, to the students and faculty at UT Dallas, through social and recreational events. Moreover, ASA shall bring awareness to the Armenian Community of UT Dallas and foster valuable communication among the Armenian students regardless of political affiliations.

Title: Arters and Crafters of UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: We support a community of artists seeking to start an independent business. By promoting and supporting a variety of different art mediums, we give artists the confidence they need to get invested in art, improve their skills, and potentially start an independent career in art.

Title: Artificial Intelligence Society
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The Artificial Intelligence Society is one of the largest computer science organizations at the University of Texas at Dallas. We are a team of passionate individuals, devoted to bridging the gap between people's awareness and the world of AI. Our mission is to promote artificial intelligence education at UT Dallas and in the surrounding communities.

We have placed educational outreach as one of our main priorities, to inspire others to learn more about AI past the resources our organization provides. Regardless of age, major, or experience, we believe that a simple appreciation of artificial intelligence will become necessary as this technology advances. If you stick with us, we promise to replace your fear with passion, and complexity with simplicity.

Title: ArtSci Convergence
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: "ArtSci Convergence is a student organization where arts and humanities interact with emerging science and technology. We aim to create a forum where artists, scientists, engineers, business, and technology enthusiasts can meet, exchange ideas, influence, and/or collaborate. "

Title: Ascend
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission:  drive workplace and societal impact by developing and elevating all Asian and Pacific Islander (API) business leaders and empowering them to become catalysts for change
Purpose: 
Organization Description: Ascend is the largest Pan-Asian business professional membership organization in North America. Ascend offers impactful career lifecycle and cross-industry leadership programs and networks that foster community and engagement for Pan-Asian business professionals and students, corporate partners, and the community at large.

Title: ASCM UT Dallas Student Forum
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: ASCM UT Dallas Student Forum mission is to enhance our member's experience outside of the classroom on their journey to becoming excellent supply chain professionals. We plan on doing this by providing our members valuable insights through networking opportunities, knowledge on current supply chain trends, providing knowledge regarding ASCM certifications, and professional development opportunities from events hosted by ASCM UT Dallas Student Forum.

Title: Asian Artists Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
AAA’s objective, upon establishment, is to create a new space for marginalized individuals of Asian-American, Pacific Islander, or similar descent to gather and converse, while aiding the development of their skills as artists. 
This organization serves to function as a community as opposed to an on-campus club or a campus-affiliated organization, in which artists from the Dallas area and beyond can continue to grow and help others. AAA seeks to fill the niche between an asian-student organization and career oriented clubs where artists can collaborate and improve their craft, while also making connections and developing their career paths. This organization also acts as a safe space for queer and gender non-conforming interaction for artists.

Title: Asian Student Organization
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The University of Texas at Dallas consists of one of the most diverse student populations in the state. We noticed students from smaller ethnic backgrounds join larger organizations because they do not have clubs representing their own culture. We welcome the idea of different lifestyles taking part in one another’s traditions, but we also want everyone to feel included. Our devotion to diversity and cultural inclusion is why we wanted to start the Asian Student Organization.

Title: Aspiring Creators
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Exposing talented individuals.

Title: Association for Computing Machinery
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: We're a team of students with one goal: building a greater, more collaborative computing community at The University of Texas at Dallas. We offer several semester-long programs to help UTD students hone their computer science skills including Projects, Research, Mentor Program, and Technical Interview Prep (TIP). We also have teams that work with our sponsors, team-bonding events, create our marketing, and run HackUTD, the largest hackathon in Texas and the central time zone.

Title: Association for Information Systems
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: AIS promotes excellence in tech fields through providing members professional & technical workshops, access to industry speakers, spaces to network, and opportunities for internships. We aim to inspire students to find their passion and exist to help students accomplish that.

Title: Association of Brazilian Students
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: ABS aims to offer a space where Brazilians can reconnect with their culture and find community, where non Brazilians can learn about what Brazil has to offer, and where both can engage in and enjoy all aspects of Brazilian culture.

Title: Association of Digital Music Creators
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The Association of Digital Music Creators (ADMC) is an organization where those who are interested in creating digital music have a community. If you are interested in music production, audio engineering, beat making, songwriting, vocalist, artists, bassists, drummer, pianist, etc.… If you are inclined to make any type of song or any piece of music on a DAW this is the place.
Our mission is to create a community where people feel comfortable sharing their ideas with one another. A place where people don't discriminate between bad or good ideas rather they just build off of one another. A community where people work together to create innovative, unique sounding projects learning along the way with the help of peers around them.
The purpose of this organization is for those people who are curious and want to learn more about the field of music production and digital music to have a community. It's for the people who have ideas but need help bringing them to life sonically, or for those who simply want to be apart of a creative environment. We believe that as musicians we can learn more and discover new grounds a lot quicker working together than we can working by ourselves, and the growth that can come with that is the purpose of this organization

Title: Association of Energy Engineers
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The AEE is committed to providing a platform for anyone interested in sustainability. We aim to allow students with this common ground to collaborate, socialize and share ideas and such regarding energy management, and do so through meaningful projects, collaborative events and networking with industry professionals. We are open to every and any major, and strive to serve our students with a fun way to share the topic of sustainability together.

Title: Association of International Energy Negotiators
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our mission is to provide students with valuable networking resources and opportunities for learning more about our energy and oil industry.

Title: Association of Latino Professionals For America
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: ALPFA creates opportunities, adds value, builds relationships for its members, the community, and its business partners while expanding Latino leadership in the global workforce.

Title: Association of Women in Psychology

Title: Astronomy and Cosmology Society

Title: Awaazein

Title: Baha'i Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The purpose of this club shall be to further the tenets of the Baha'i Faith, to invite those interested to investigate the faith for themselves; to promote the unity of mankind; and to foster understanding, love and fellowship on this campus by sponsoring such things as lectures, information tables, service projects, discussions, informal gatherings, social activities, and public meetings. This club shall not endorse any partisan or political group on a campus level or otherwise, and shall remain not-for-profit add non-commercial in nature.

Title: Baking for a Cause
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our mission is to bake desserts to donate to local homeless shelters while also having fun trying out new recipes together. All skill levels are welcome. We hope to foster an inclusive, welcoming environment where members can have a chance to bond with one another and have fun baking!

Title: Bangladeshi Student Organization

The mission of the Black Graduate Student Association is to promote an environment for Black students and students of African descent to facilitate and cultivate a safe space and establish a community that bolsters the advancement of Black representation throughout all professional realms. 

The purpose of the BGSA is to foster a community amongst Black graduates and professional students at The University of Texas at Dallas. Graduate students may express ideas, concerns, and grievances in a safe space. This organization shall create opportunities for social activities, leadership, professional development, and mentoring among the University of Texas at Dallas community and surrounding city. Title: Black Students for Public Health
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission: The mission of Black Students for Public Health at the University of Texas at Dallas is to educate, prepare and provide underrepresented health students with hands-on experience to tackle such health disparities through workshops, community engagements, and networking opportunities that will promote public health equity and racial justice in our community.


Purpose:
The purpose of this organization is to create an environment for underrepresented minority students to succeed as future Public Health leaders in order to cultivate and foster a space to address the structural inequalities within the healthcare system through public health initiatives. As a means of acting on this purpose, the organization will provide the following resources:
						
● Networking
○ Building and cultivating relationships between members and their peers as a			
means to develop a strong support system; also, provide opportunities for students to grow their professional network with public health personnels.
						
● Workshops
○ Provide educational and interactive sessions for students to grow their knowledge in public health literacy, partake in pertinent group activities, and foster fruitful dialogues amongst like-minded students.
						
● Community Engagements				
○ Introduce students to hands-on opportunities and exposure into dealing with the public health crisis that negatively impacts the black community. Also, partaking in community and local-based interventions in the DFW area as a means to reduce health inequalities in underserved communities.

Description:

The name of this organization shall be called: Black Students for Public Health and/or referred to as “BSP”. The official colors of this organization shall be: Black, White, and Periwinkle Blue

Purpose: To help its members develop important soft skills such as teamwork and analytical thinking skills in a unique and fun way.

Title: Bujo Buddies UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Description: Bujo Buddies UTD is an organization centered around the hobby of bullet journaling, which is a beloved organizational tool and creative outlet that allows people to express their artistic talents through drawing, scrapbooking, doodling, and the use of fun stationery such as washi tape and stickers, while also being a resource to plan out important tasks in a given month, week, and year overall. Bullet journaling encourages the idea of "spreads", which is a creative layout of everything planned for a certain time period. For example, creating a monthly spread would be decorating a cover page dedicated to the month's themes, and then creating a calendar and filling in important dates and deadlines within it. A weekly spread would be taking one week from the monthly calendar and focusing in on it more to highlight details on what is important for that given week. Bullet journaling appeals to those who are artistic and want to express themselves through beautiful calligraphy and stationery like stickers, cutouts, washi tape, colored pens, and more, but it also appeals to those who want a more organizational and functional form of planning. Outside of bullet journaling, our organization will also plan activities related to bullet journaling in some form such as calligraphy workshops, pen palling events, stationery swaps, and more. Most importantly, Bujo Buddies UTD emphasizes inclusion and friendship. We want everyone in our organization to feel comfortable and included as our members, and we want everyone to have their voices heard in terms of what type of events they would like to see. Whether one has been bullet journaling for years or is interested in getting started, Bujo Buddies UTD will strive to bring everyone together and bullet journal in order to encourage accountability and productivity in a cute and artistic manner.

Mission: The mission of Bujo Buddies UTD is to create a positive, friendly space for those interested in bullet journaling to come together and share ideas for bullet journaling spreads and explore the art of aesthetics, productivity, and organization in a medium which promotes creativity, design, and self care. Our purpose is to allow students to view bullet journaling as an stress-reliving organizational tool which only requires a space to express themselves, such as digital or traditional journal, and their own brilliant ideas. We seek to unite students in making planning their busy college lives more fun through friendships and bujo!

Title: CHAARG at UTD

Title: Chabad Jewish Student Organization at UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The primary purpose of the Chabad Jewish Association is to provide living Judaism to the students at UTD within the framework of traditional Judaism.  The club falls under the SOC categories of Religious, Educational, and Cultural.  Chabad serves individuals looking for a warm, non-judgmental, accepting, personalized Jewish experience. We are dedicated to providing every student, regardless of background, philosophy, or level of commitment, an open door environment for strengthening and enhancing Jewish life and connection. Our objectives include but are not limited to; educational, ritual, social activities, counseling, and spiritual guidance to individual students. Our organization is based on Chassidic philosophy, as taught locally by the Chabad Jewish Center, directed by Rabbi Shmuly and Dini Druk. Our philosophy states that every Jew deserves an equal opportunity to experience and enjoy their heritage. All programs and events of the CJSA are within the framework of Halachah, Jewish law as transmitted by the Rabbis throughout the generations dating back to Moses receiving the Torah at Mount Sinai.

Title: Changing Health Attitudes and Actions to Recreate GIrls
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The purpose of our organization is to "free girls from the elliptical" meaning that the form of exercise that is advertised towards women are forms of cardio, as a result, a lot of girls don't know how to lift weights or aren't educated about other forms of exercise. Our organization aims to introduce a variety of exercises to girls as well as teach them how to start their fitness journey.

Title: Chi Phi Fraternity
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Our fraternity is more than just an organization of men, it is a brotherhood bound together by ritual and shared belief in bettering one another. These are the men who will be your Best Man, who you will go through life with together. To further the bonds of this brotherhood, Chi Phi hosts numerous events throughout the semester exclusively for brothers. These encompass a wide range of activities: paintball, tubing, weekly lunches on Friday (known as Chi Phriday to our members) to name a few. Planned by the Brotherhood Committee, these events provide wonderful opportunities for brothers to relax after hard weeks, catch up with one another, and have fun!

Our chapter believes that every new member should have someone that they are close to beyond what a large chapter can offer. Each new member gets a big brother before being initiated to help them with everything from fraternity life to school work. The big brother acts as a mentor and a best friend when they are in need. From this bond we build a lineage of strongly bound members that will often adopt the name of their founding brother. The big brothers are chosen shortly before initiation based on the personal relations of current members to the new members. We aim to have close connections between the brother to truly give them a sense of belonging in the chapter.

Mission -
To make students well versed with coding languages and prepare them for various competitions and future job roles.

1. To organize weekly sessions at the university for students to learn new technologies.
2. To conduct workshops between students and industry professionals regarding new tools and
technologies used in the industry.

share and enhance knowledge in the field of Information Technology.

Organization Description - 
This organization believes technology can transform the world, and the road to transformative technology goes most of the time through programming or coding. Our organization hopes to make the transition of students with no coding background towards programming-related subjects easy, or if you already are an expert, we have exciting events for you.
Code.exe strives to create a roadmap for students based on the philosophy of learning, practice, and win. We conduct boot camps, expert speaker sessions, mentoring sessions, and hackathons to help students win at coding. As we are based in JSOM, currently, we are exploring R and Python for analytics, and you will see exciting content and events related to the same.

Title: College Diabetes Network at UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Hello, and thank you for your interest in College Diabetes Network at UTD! 

Over 1.25 million people in the United States have Type 1 Diabetes. Type 1 Diabetes is a chronic, autoimmune disease that cannot be prevented and currently has no cure. 

Who we are: College Diabetes Network at UTD is a collection of students with and without Type 1 Diabetes who strive to increase Type 1 Diabetes awareness and education on campus. 

Our mission: Our chapter of the College Diabetes Network was founded for the purpose of connecting students with and affected by Type 1 Diabetes in hopes that these connections will create a support network which will enable students to fulfill their potential and limit the negative psychological and physical effects of Type 1 Diabetes. 

Please contact us at utdallas@collegediabetesnetwork.org for more information on our upcoming meetings! 

Follow us on Facebook or instagram @cdnutd!

Title: College Panhellenic Council
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The College Panhellenic Council (CPC), or Panhellenic, is UT Dallas’ coordinating body of the four National Panhellenic Conference sororities on campus. The council is composed of six executive board members and a delegate from each sorority. The College Panhellenic Executive council consists of members who are current sorority women who have disaffiliated from their chapters during recruitment to provide unbiased planning and support.
The College Panhellenic Council is dedicated to creating an environment in which women going through the recruitment process feel welcomed. It is our priority to take all necessary precautions to keep our members and those who are going through the recruitment process healthy and safe.

Title: Collegiate Chapter of the Association of Fundraising Professionals at UT Dallas (AFP UTD)
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Collegiate Chapter of the Association of Fundraising Professionals at UT Dallas (AFP UTD) is the first student-led organization at UT Dallas for students and young professionals who are interested in fundraising. The UTD AFP Collegiate Chapter is also part of something much bigger - a worldwide professional organization that not only is a part of a global network of fundraising professionals across the globe, but also a catalyst for an amazing career. 

The Chapter is supported by a faculty advisor (Dr. Elizabeth Searing), a local AFP chapter Professional Advisor (Dr. Holly Miori), and a UTD PNM student/Collegiate Chapter President (Thanh Hoang).
 
AFP UTD collegiate chapter will:
- Connect students and young professionals sharing passion in nonprofit management and fundraising
- Provide learning opportunities such as webinar, fundraising conference, internship and other training opportunities
- Prepare students for future career through mentoring, leadership opportunity, and wide network

Title: CometTutors

We hope to bridge the gap in education between students and help provide consistency in the lives of students who may lack a guiding voice in their academic careers. 

The purpose of CometTutors is to:
Help schools in the Greater Dallas area by allowing select college students to tutor high school students in the AVID program in relevant math and science subjects as well as provide college/life advice. Also to Allow students to make a long-lasting impact in the community by understanding how to teach and mentor younger peers. 

CometTutors is a branch of the nonprofit Promoting Education Across the Country (PEAC). The organization offers tutoring and mentoring services at local high schools and middle schools. The organization also offers extra services such as educational videos, scholarships, and SAT/ACT test prep.

Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The mission of the Criminology Graduate Student Organization is to provide it's members professional development opportunities, networking opportunities, and resources to excel in graduate school.

Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Connect students that are interested in Criminology and provide them with educational and social opportunities including socials and guest speakers.

Title: Cru
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Cru states that their mission is that every student at UTD would have a chance to know God, grow in their faith, and go to the world. 

This vision is employed through various social events, bible studies, and mission trip opportunities.

Title: Cybersecurity Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Cybersecurity Club is a student organization that strives to spread awareness and promote knowledge about Information technology Security, a niche but vast field of study from management perspective.

Title: Dallas Formula Racing
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Dallas Formula Racing is a student-led organization that designs, builds, and competes a formula-style race car each year for the international Formula SAE competition. Students who participate in the Formula SAE competition are sought out by employers because they are aware of the high skill level and experience of these students. We are open to all students at any skill level or major, and we will teach the skills needed to be successful on the team.

Title: Dallas Formula Racing
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our goal is to cultivate well-rounded engineers by fostering technical skills, teamwork, and business acumen through the entire engineering and design cycle. We strive to challenge and inspire students to pursue their passions while giving them a platform to showcase their abilities through participation in the Formula SAE competition.

Title: Dallas Musical Outreach
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Dallas Musical Outreach is a music service organization that works to spread the joy and spirit of music throughout the DFW metroplex! Through performances, volunteerism, and philanthropy, DMO serves the UT Dallas campus and Greater Dallas community. Dallas Musical Outreach specifically gives UTD students an outlet for musical expression and raises awareness for music in all spaces, funds for local charities and low-income music programs, and spirits of UTD’s students and audiences everywhere DMO ensembles perform.

Title: Data Science Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Data Science Club is a student organization at The University of Texas at Dallas that strives to be the single focal point of overall development and growth of the student community with interests in the areas of machine learning, data mining and artificial intelligence. 
DSC is connecting the dots to build a networked society of students, industry professionals, faculty, and companies. It is a testament to the ongoing year-round activities which are tailor-made to suit the current and future needs of students and industries. DSC helps you stay updated with the latest trends and industry best practices. It also helps fine-tune your technical and professional skills and test yourself through flagship data science competitions. Above all, you get boundless networking opportunities and make yourself market-ready!

Title: Dauntless
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
We are a service organization that strives to:
- Promote Mental Health
- Raise Awareness for the Victims of Domestic Violence

Title: DECA
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: DECA is an educational organization that prepares emerging leaders and entrepreneurs in marketing, finance, hospitality, and management. Our purpose is to provide opportunities to simulate specific industry challenges in 25 different types of competitive events. DECA has many chapters in high schools and colleges around the globe to build our future leaders.

Title: Deeds Not Words
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Deeds not words is an intersectional community that stands for women’s social and economic opportunity, access to reproductive health, freedom from sexual assault, and equal representation in every space and at every level. Led by YOUth, we’re uniting women to push progress forward — not through talk. Through deeds.

Title: Delta Delta Delta
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: "The purpose of Delta Delta Delta shall be to establish a perpetual bond of friendship among its members, to develop a stronger and more womanly character, to broaden the moral and intellectual life, and to assist its members in every possible way. It shall also be the purpose of Delta Delta Delta to promote and develop mutually beneficial relationships between the Fraternity and the colleges and universities where the Fraternity has established chapters, to develop qualities of unselfish leadership among its members, and to encourage them to assume, with integrity and devotion to moral and democratic principles, the highest responsibilities of college women."

Title: Delta Epsilon Psi
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Service and Social South Asian Fraternity

Delta Epsilon Psi is driven by the three pillars of Brotherhood, Discipline, and Commitment.

The first conceptualized vision of Delta Epsilon Psi Fraternity, Inc. was conceived fourteen years ago at The University of Texas at Dallas. Twelve motivated, South Asian young men developed this vision into existence. These men are now considered the founding fathers of the Beta Chapter of Delta Epsilon Psi Fraternity, Inc. They first set out with the goal of establishing a legitimate South Asian presence around campus at The University of Texas at Dallas. They strived to do this by utilizing the three pillars of Brotherhood, Discipline, and Commitment. This idea became a reality when the founding class of Delta Epsilon Psi Beta Chapter was officially recognized on August 6, 2000. At that moment Delta Epsilon Psi became the very first South Asian Fraternity on the campus.Once past the infancy stage, Delta Epsilon Psi began a phase of nationwide expansion. It began with other southern schools like Baylor University and University of South Florida. This rapid expansion marks the success of our fraternity by being so well supported that the small idea of eighteen young men has now developed into a nationwide operation to bring together South Asian students under the same fundamental ideas and philosophies.While Delta Epsilon Psi has grown to boast an impressive national presence, the Beta Chapter in Dallas holds to the roots it was established upon Fourteen years ago. This commitment has allowed us to maintain our position as one of Delta Epsilon Psi’s elite chapters.

Title: Delta Kappa Delta Sorority
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: We are the first and only south asian based service sorority and are committed to our philanthropy which is the prevention and awareness of child abuse!

Title: Delta Sigma Pi - Chi Psi
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: As the world’s leading professional fraternity for men and women, Delta Sigma Pi develops principled business leaders for the future by providing a lifetime of opportunity for our members. Since 1907, our values and mission have guided our commitment to scholarship, service and professionalism. Delta Sigma Pi members, who are as diverse as the business world, gain experiences in professionalism, leadership, ethics, and philanthropy. This all leads to personal development and success—both in the business world and the civic realm. Delta Sigma Pi doesn’t just develop professionals; the Fraternity provides members with relationships, skills, and ideals necessary for them to make a significant impact wherever life leads. Delta Sigma Pi is a lifetime commitment that has a lifetime of opportunities.

Title: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is an organization of college educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.

Title: Delta Tau Delta
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Iota Omega chapter of Delta Tau Delta at the University of Texas at Dallas

Title: Delta Zeta - Pi Sigma
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The women of Delta Zeta Sorority share a common purpose: to make life fuller, more meaningful, and more rewarding. Within Delta Zeta, we call it enriching. To our members, it is the promise of friendship, personal development, and opportunity. To the campus community and the world, we are valued citizens who are deeply committed to making a difference where we live. Our promise to our Sorority and our commitment to enrich the world is for a lifetime. Enriching is more than a brand to Delta Zeta; it is a promise.

Title: Design Build Fly
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Design Build Fly (DBF) is an international aeronautical design competition hosted by the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA). Every year, over one hundred university teams participate to design, build, and fly a large remote-controlled aircraft to perform three flight missions and one ground mission. At the University of Texas at Dallas (UTD), we are an interdisciplinary team of aspiring aerospace engineers tasked with producing an aircraft to represent the school at the competition. We provide hands-on, technical experiences by combining aeronautical, mechanical, electrical, and other engineering processes into a competitive design project. Students of all majors and years are welcome to join!

Title: Developer Student Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Developer Student Clubs are community groups for college and university students interested in Google developer technologies.

Students from all undergraduate or graduate programs with an interest in growing as a developer are welcome. By joining a DSC, students grow their knowledge in a peer-to-peer learning environment and build solutions for local businesses and their community.

Developer Student Clubs is a program supported by Google Developers.

Title: Dhunki A Cappella
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: In Hindi, ‘Dhunki’ means ‘spellbound.’ The name also contains the Hindi word ‘Dhun,’ which means ‘tune’ or ‘melody.’ This Hindi name symbolizes this group’s focus on the areas of South Asian music and culture. Our goal is to spread our culture throughout the community by the means of a unique style of music in which Eastern and Western themes collide in intricately arranged mashups.

Title: Divergent
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Divergent is an organization that aims to help people understand different neurodevelopmental disorders. The organization will help students understand the potential causes of and cures for these conditions, and will encourage them to help foster a climate of acceptance and empathy towards those who have been affected. The organization’s mission is to create a new generation of individuals who will make the world a better place for children with neurodevelopmental disorders.

Title: Effective Altruism UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Effective Altruism UT Dallas is a special interest group that aims to promote effective altruism in all its forms, especially the idea of using one’s career to improve the world. Effective Altruism UT Dallas will explain the ideas of effective altruism and provide support to our members on how to put those ideas into practice.

Title: Environmental Conservation Organization at UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Promoting and educating care for the environment and sustainability through volunteering and on-campus initiatives.

Title: Envision
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Envision is a Student Club in which our goal is to make everyone aware of the various visualization tools which are currently used in every industry and how to leverage that in their day-to-day job and subsequently increase efficiency. Our organization's values are to be able to give the best learning experience to the students outside their curriculum and we hope to reach the maximum audience in doing so. We support all members and students in getting the right resources needed to learn various data visualization tools there is.

Title: Epic Movement
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission:
- Winning - Winning believers to the Body of Christ by the presentation of the Gospel.
- Building - Building one another in Christ and also as a Christ-centered community. 
- Sending - Sending our brothers and sisters to  spread the Gospel whether in their respective career fields or in the world as missionaries.

Vision: The vision of UTD Epic is to cultivate a Christ-centered, multiplying community of Asian American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) students who are living on mission to reach their campus, city, and the world.

Description: Epic is an Asian-American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) Christian ministry organization which is living on mission to reach their campus, city, and the world. We invite all those who have lived and are interested in learning about Christianity through AAPI lenses.

Title: FASHION CLUB
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Fashion Club is a safe haven for creativity and self-expression through fashion. Our mission is to offer a platform for designers and models to express their talents. We work on connecting members with fashion professionals, teach those interested about the fashion industry, and host social events.

Title: FeelGood UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our philanthropy is to end world hunger by 2030. We aim to raise awareness for our cause on campus and sell food to raise money, of which 100% of the proceeds go to The Hunger Project (our partner non-profit).

Title: Fellowship of Christian University Students
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: It shall be the purpose of Fellowship of Christian University Students to create a place for both Christian and Non-Christian UTD students to build relationships and to learn about Christ, Mission: Making and Maturing Disciples on DFW College Campuses, org description: Jesus also said no one should follow Him by themselves, so as a community, we are involved in each other’s lives.  We gather every week in student-led small groups called Cores where we share life, pray, and read the Bible together.  All of us in Core have the opportunity to meet regularly with a student leader to help us grow in following Jesus well.  Those student leaders are mentored by a Campus Pastor who helps pastor our community.

Title: Female Leaders of America in Science and Health
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Female Leaders of America in Science and Health (FLASH) is a service and educational organization that will serve as a platform for students that are interested in the science fields. It will foster well-rounded students by informing, encouraging, supporting and educating them throughout their undergraduate years. The organization will guide students through graduate and professional school admission test prep, the application process, tutoring, First Aid, CPR and Stop the Bleed Certifications, volunteering and shadowing opportunities, and more. The organization will also inspire all genders to combat the many challenges women face around the world, such as inadequate resources for menstrual hygiene, improper access to education, inequality in the workforce, etc., to work towards a world where gender doesn't restrict anyone's growth. The organization’s mission is to create a generation of health professionals that are empowered to serve the world beyond their healthcare professions.

Title: Filipino Student Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The purpose of The Filipino Student Association is to promote Filipino culture while creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for UTD students and non-UTD students.

Title: Financial Leadership Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
UTD Financial Leadership Association is focused on developing students to become future business leaders. FLA prepares students to excel in their future careers through competitions, networking events, career development workshops and meetings with industry leaders.


FLA teaches students how to get ahead by preparing them in every aspect of the industry in an environment of like-minded peers and professionals.

Title: FinTech UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission: We hope to allow students to explore the specific constructs and implementations of capital market modeling, modern digital payment infrastructure, expansion of robo-advisors and industry insights on the sector.
Purpose: Our organization is dedicated to the study and development of products and solutions in the financial technology space.
Organization Description: We are a student organization that aims to educate students about various ideas in the FinTech space and practically apply these concepts through hands-on long-term projects.

Title: First Generation Students of STEM
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The First Generation Students in the STEM organization strives to offer additional help to the students by providing social activities, volunteering opportunities, and clinical experiences.

Title: First Love Fellowship
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission : Our mission is to encourage students to be Christians who strife for academic excellence and success in life through
biblical principles.
Purpose:
The purpose of First Love Fellowship on UTD campus is
1. To provide an environment on campus where UTD students meet and support each other in their
Christian walk through mutual fellowship.
2. To train members to become Christian and global leaders.
3. To introduce the greater love of Christ Jesus to students
4. To reach out to students of University Of Texas at Dallas (UTD), to help them to unearth their
potential and God given gifts making them productive and accountable citizens in school and any
subsequent community in which they find themselves.

Title: Fit Life UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
We are a club founded on the principle of promoting health and fitness by serving and educating the community, hosting events that encourage members to maintain a healthy lifestyle, and fundraising events which allows others to have access to equipment and facilities to maintain their health.

By spreading awareness through many platforms such as in-person fitness events, group runs, hikes, and workouts, and social media we can pique the public’s interest in our mission. Giving back to the community is another piece of what this organization represents. Our organization will be hosting events in which canned food drives, supply drives, and volunteer work options are going to be available for all members who want to make a difference within themselves and a difference in the community we all live in.

We believe that empowering individuals through these avenues together, as one unity, is a very impactful way to raise awareness for the need of maintaining general health.

Title: For Autistic Empowerment
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
As an organization, we have two general goals:
1. Building a social community for neurodiverse and interested neurotypical students on campus. We have regular social meetings, which oftentimes include a discussion of challenges we face and our ideas for overcoming these challenges. We additionally have frequent social hangouts, such as movie or game nights!
2. Extending our experiences to the broader community. We have spoken with autistic students, their parents, and even educator groups- to describe our personal experiences, and how we've learned to navigate life's challenges!

Title: Foster Connect
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Foster Connect is a service organization that serves to support the needs of foster youth that are transitioning into, currently under, and transitioning out of the care of the Texas Department of Family and Protective Services (DFPS). 

Foster Connect has

Title: Fraternity & Sorority Life
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Fraternity & Sorority Life at UT Dallas is young, refreshing, and entrepreneurial. The chapters, like the university, are vibrantly diverse, academically proud, and are actively preparing their members for successful careers. We have been inspired by the history of our organizations across North America, but we are driven by the modern needs of today's students. The UT Dallas Fraternity and Sorority community seeks to promote, develop, and enhance the pillars that our community prides itself on: Belonging, Opportunity, and Personal Development. We would love for you to experience something that no one else at UT Dallas offers: 

The opportunity to invent tradition daily.

Title: Friends of MSF at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Description: Friends of MSF at UT Dallas is a student chapter of Doctors Without Borders, we are an organization based on volunteering and fundraising for the purpose of both giving support to the international organization and doing our part in impacting our local community in a positive way.

​Our mission is to prove that even the smallest act of compassion can make a huge difference when we all come together.

Purpose: The purpose of this organization is to advocate and raise money for Doctors Without
Borders. Many college students are unaware of what Doctors Without Borders is and what they
do. DWB is based in France and works in more than 60 countries, providing medical care where
it is needed the most. DWB helps with medical crises caused by war, natural disaster, poverty,
and epidemics. Many lives have been saved by DWB, and I want to give the UT Dallas
community an opportunity to help such a great and necessary cause. The UT Dallas chapter of
DWB will also volunteer around the community and have social events for its members.

Title: Friendship Association of Chinese Students and Scholars
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Friendship Association of Chinese Students and Scholars (FACSS) is the leading Chinese student organization at UT Dallas. With 5 different branches and more than 50 active members, FACSS is also one of the biggest student organizations on campus. Every summer, FACSS collaborates with other school organizations, such as ICP and Big Howdy, to provide help and services for incoming Chinese students at UTD, such as free airport pick-up and local-help. Each semester, FACSS holds various events, such as new student orientation, Halloween party, and performances on Mid-Autumn Day and Chinese New Year, to enrich campus life and promote diversity across the campus. Meanwhile, FACSS always communicates with the school about the concerns and needs of Chinese students at UTD and tries its best to provide needed help.

Title: Future Academic Mentorship
Category: Educational/Departmental, Academic Interest, Office of Student Volunteerism, Student Leadership
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Future Admission Mentorship promotes UTD students to give back to the community by helping high school students plan their next phase of life. This includes anything from needing help with how to study for classes or SATs, applying to colleges, providing other valuable options besides colleges, and guidance for what major would be best for them.

Title: Future Healthcare Leaders Alliance (FHLA)
Category: Academic Interest, Social, Service, Educational/Departmental, Office of Student Volunteerism, Student Leadership, Special Interest
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission : Future Health Leaders Alliance is a pre-health organization that aims to provide members with a deeper level of insight into different healthcare specialties and to allow members to develop and enhance important basic skills utilized in the various healthcare fields.  

Purpose: The purpose of Future Healthcare Leaders Alliance (FHLA) is to unite all pre-health fields in a collaborative environment where sharing ideas and opportunities is encouraged and students can investigate which field is the best fit for them. For students who have already selected their desired career path, there are other opportunities such as volunteering, shadowing, research, and more to help him / her become the best applicant for that particular post-graduate school. 

Organization Description: The place to be for student who want to go into a healthcare related field but are unsure of which field in particular. It caters to the needs of all (Pre-Med, Pre-Dental, Pre-Physical / Occupational Therapy, Pre-Physician Assistant, Pre-Nursing, Pre-Optometry, etc.) students at the University of Texas at Dallas

Title: Galerstein Gender Center
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The Galerstein Gender Center (GGC), under the Office of Diversity and Community Engagement, has been in existence since 1996. We advocate and support women, LGBT+ people and all communities facing oppression. Our programs are intersectional and delivered through educational workshops, lectures, leadership development, advising, resource referral, celebratory programs and advocacy. We also offer volunteer opportunities and organizational advising.

In the GGC you will find meeting and study space and the GGC Resource Collection, which contains a number of books on feminism and gender diversity. We are also home to the first dedicated lactation space on campus for parents who are nursing. We invite you to visit the GGC and find out how you can become involved.

Title: Gamma Iota Sigma
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Gamma lota Sigma (GIS) is a registered 501 (c)(3) educational organization founded in 1965, and is the only international business fraternity for students of insurance, risk management and actuarial science. GIS is open to ALL majors. GIS pursues a mission to promote and encourage student interest in the industry as a profession while encouraging the high moral and scholastic attainments of its members. Through world-class programs, innovative partnerships, critical industry support, expansive campus engagement, and an unrivaled lifelong professional network, GIS leads the sustainable growth and diversification of the insurance industry's student talent pipeline across all functional areas.

GIS Beta Phi Chapter at The University of Texas at Dallas was chartered in March, 2016. Our organization helps students learn about the industry, network with insurance professionals, and eventually find iobs and internshios

Title: Gamma Theta Upsilon Geographic Honors Society
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
1.To further professional interest in geography by affording a common organization for those interested in this field.
2.To  strengthen  student and professional training through academic experiences other than those of the classroom and laboratory.
3.To advance the status of geography as a cultural and practical discipline for study and investigation.
4.To encourage student research of high quality and to provide an outlet for publication.
5.To create and administer funds for furthering graduate study and/or research in the field of geography.
6.To encourage members to apply geographic knowledge and skills in service to humankind.

Title: GDI Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The Global Development Initiative focuses on developing market entry strategies for international companies and aims to equip university students with the necessary skills to succeed and navigate through the complex global business environment.

The purpose of the organization is to diffuse knowledge of the international business consulting industry and the related domains. We will achieve this purpose by organizing guest speaker events, competitions, seminars, and activities which would allow students to gain hands-on experience with Global Development Initiative.

Title: Geoclub
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Act as a support network for geoscience students at UTD.

Title: geophysical society
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Geophysics, which uses mathematics and physical tool to quantitatively analyze the Earth, is one important branch of the Earth's science. The organization is grouped up by people who engage in, or are interested in, Geophysics. The society of Exploration Geophysicists (SEG) is one of the largest academic and industrial organizations around the world. As the student chapter of the SEG at the University of Texas at Dallas, we associate the students in UTD to attend activities held by SEG, acquire resources from SEG, and acknowledge opportunities around SEG.

Title: Geophysical Society
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Geophysical Society brings together a group of like-minded individuals who share a common interest in geophysics. Providing opportunities for leadership, achievement, and cultivation of ideas, SEG Student Chapters open the door for students to actively participate in projects and initiatives that promote the science of geophysics.

Title: Girls Who Code
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our mission is to build sisterhood and friendship between members, provide a platform to empower and support women in STEM, and learn technical and professional skills together as a sisterhood.

Title: Global Business Organization
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission: GBO is a student organization that allows an area for students interested in business, world culture, and world affairs to interact and network with each other through club activities including volunteering opportunities, international and domestic case competitions, and guest speaker events.

Purpose/Vision: GBO strives to create a platform for all students to better their understanding of business cultures internationally and prepare them for the challenges and the opportunities presented in them while building a community between students via networking.

Title: Global Business Organization
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Open to all Undergraduate and Graduate Students
International Trade
Develop professional skills and guidance in the field of Global Business
Give students knowledge about other cultures, countries, and business practices

Title: Global Medical and Dental Brigades
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our mission is to empower volunteers and under-resourced communities to resolve global health and economic disparities and inspire all involved to collaboratively work towards an equal world. Global Brigades at the University of Texas at Dallas is a secular, international student-operated volunteer organization whose mission is to provide sustainable development to underserved communities throughout the world.  It will offer 1 Brigade Program: Medical/Dental. Along with affiliate groups at other distinguished universities, our University of Texas at Dallas team recruits student volunteers and professionals, gathers supplies, and then travels overseas to set up free medical and dental clinics and run educational programs to improve public health and sanitation, in a variety of underserved global communities. Students will visit several community projects that aim to also better the quality of life in these impoverished areas.

Title: Global Medical Missions Alliance
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Vision: For students interested in healthcare to develop a heart to lead a missional life wherever they go
Mission Statements: 

To equip students with a holistic view on care by providing short-term medical mission opportunities
To offer meaningful, impactful and consistent volunteering opportunities for students.
To connect students with their peers and Christian mentors who encourage missional living in each other’s careers

Title: Global Mobility Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
To bring awareness of the Global Mobility Industry to UT Dallas and students at the Naveen Jindal School of Management. 
To support and educate various professional goals of Global Business students (undergraduate and masters) and the Center of Global Business

Title: Global Surgery Student Alliance
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The purpose of the organization shall be to act as an interdisciplinary forum for students with a strong interest in Global Surgery, to network, discuss, advocate for and participate in research and educational opportunities within the essential field of Global Surgery. Through our alliances with the national Global Surgery Student Association , we seek to promote awareness for sustainable and accessible surgical care worldwide by educating, and uniting students through engagement and mentorship opportunities in Global Surger

Title: Golden Key Honor Society
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Golden Key Honor Society here at the University of Texas at Dallas is a chapter of the Golden Key International Honor Society. Golden Key's mission is to enable members to realize their potential through the advancement of academics, leadership, and service. Members qualify when they are in the top 15% of their class.

The purpose of our club is to provide personal, leadership, and career development programs, as well as education-based service programs.

Title: Golden Z Club at The University of Texas at Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Golden Z Club stands for women's rights and advocates for equality, education, diversifying cultural aspects, and ending child marriage and gender-based violence through international education programs service projects. This organization is dedicated to creating a supportive global community of like-minded professionals from diverse countries and cultures and offers a variety of scholarships (aerospace, business, technology, public affairs) to its members.

Title: Grace Chinese Christian Fellowship
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
We are a group of Chinese International students from Mainland China, Taiwan, Malaysia, Hong Kong, and other countries who are interested in studying the bible and learning about Christianity in the Mandarin Chinese language. We consist primarily of UTD students, professors, alumni, and other professionals, both Christians and non-Christians.

We seek to provide everyone with an easy-going, joyful, warm, and family-like bible studying environment. We do not believe in pressuring people to believe what we do. We believe that we are given free will to accept or reject Jesus Christ. However, through reading and studying God’s Word, we hope that everyone will come to know and accept the truth of God and to an abundant and eternal life as promised in the bible.

Title: Graduate Finance Management Council
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Graduate Finance Management Council (GFMC) is focused on the needs and interests of graduate finance students. The dedicated GFMC members serve as program ambassadors, mentors and leaders for the MS Finance program. Their support is essential for  program support and student success.  We strongly believe that our MSF alumni are assets and brand ambassadors for the MSF program as well as a great source of professional guidance for the MSF students.

Title: Graduate Students in Physics
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Graduate Students in Physics provides social and academic opportunities to the department, and works to solve issues concerning the student experience. We aim to offer support to everyone in the graduate physics program and increase the value of their time at UTD.

Title: GRADUATES OF COMPUTER SCIENCE
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: UTD GCS is an organization that is dedicated to the cause of representing the interests of the graduate students of the Department of Computer Science at UT Dallas, promoting interaction among students and faculty of the department, organizing academic and social events, and providing information pertaining to departmental activities and policies.

Title: Habesha Student Union
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our main goal as an organization is to promote Ethiopian and Eritrean culture. Every year, we have charity events, fundraisers, socials, study halls, and many more events. We look forward to growing to be a bigger organization on campus in the years to come.

Title: Hack To Help
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission:
Debunking the myth of “The Genius Programmer” and promoting social good in the process. \

Purpose:
Provide a medium for the non-CS UT Dallas community to get an intro to programming and pique their interest through web development.
making programming accessible for people of all backgrounds

Organization Description:
Hack to Help started with the goal of creating a safe space for everyone to learn programming. We work towards achieving that by hosting hands-on workshops several times a semester open to all experience levels and organize events such as speaker series to provide students with exposure to the tech industry.

Title: Hallyu Wave
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Hallyu Wave is a student organization that specializes in both Korean culture and entertainment. We are an organization that aims to provide a fun, diverse community for all students, while also providing educational meetings that incorporate group discussions, traditions and culture, dance tutorials, Korean entertainment discussions, and the Korean language.

Title: Hansini
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: UT Dallas' premiere Indian Classical Dance competition in the Southern United States, Hansini. Hansini aims to provide a platform for collegiate students all around the nation to showcase their talent and continue pursuing classical Indian dance forms.

Title: Health Occupations Students of America (HOSA)
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The mission of HOSA is to empower HOSA-Future Health Professionals to become leaders in the global health community through education, collaboration, and experience.
Our main aspect is competition where members compete in a health-related category against other HOSA members in the state and nation. We offer volunteering, fundraising, and social opportunities for our members, as well as mentorship for incoming HOSA members and pre-medical students applying for the current application cycle.

Title: Health Occupations Students of America (HOSA)
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission: The mission of HOSA is to empower Future Health Professionals to become leaders in the global health community through education, collaboration, and experience.

Purpose: HOSA's purpose is to facilitate community involvement and create meaningful experiences for our members. 

Organization Description: HOSA-Future Health Professionals is a pre-health organization at UTD.
