Rather than try to find young men to
"mold" into some ideal, we celebrate the uniqueness of each individual and, through encouragement, values, example and brotherhood, empower every brother to exceed his personal expectations.

Title: Phi Gamma Delta
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Phi Gamma Delta unites men in enduring friendships, stimulates the pursuit of knowledge, and builds courageous leaders who serve the world with the best that is in them.

Title: Philosophy Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
To foster a love of the search for wisdom, knowledge, and enlightenment.
To create a safe, open, and tranquil environment for the members.
To allow conversations of intellectual depth.
To encourage members to question the fundamental theories of their own beliefs.

Title: Photography Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The UT Dallas Photography Club serves to promote awareness and appreciation of photography within the general student body at The University of Texas at Dallas. The UT Dallas Photography Club strives to be a place where students will be able to interact, exchange ideas and experiences, and contribute to the growth of the club.

Title: Pi Kappa Phi
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: To create an uncommon and lifelong brotherhood that develops leaders and encourages service to others for the betterment of our communities. Vision Statement: A future where every Pi Kappa Phi embraces his role as a leader, puts service before self and improves the world around him.

Title: Pokemon League at UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our mission is to get people interested in the Pokémon game series, as well as having fun with it as a community. Our purpose is to have a place where people who love Pokémon can socialize with people of similar interests.

Title: Pool Club UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Provide a community/space for players of all skill levels to socialize, compete, and learn the art of billiards. In addition we also plan to host fundraisers for important world relief causes.

Title: Post Baccalaureate Pre-Health Society
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: This organization was created for the purpose of bringing together post-baccalaureate pre-health majors to gain information through public speakers, perform volunteer activities and form friendships.

Title: Pre Graduate Student Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Our organization aims to connect pre-graduate students from different fields and create a helpful 
and supportive environment for growth and education on different methods to achieve students’ 
goals in their undergraduate studies. This makes our association an educational organization. We 
plan on holding events regarding each field and go over the requirements, tips, and tricks to 
improve and build upon students’ experiences. We also plan on offering volunteer opportunities 
regarding each respective field that will give students real-life experience in their field. As 
students, we would’ve benefited from such an organization, especially in our early semesters.

Title: Pre-Dental Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The Pre-Dental Association at the University of Texas at Dallas is a student-run organization that strives to provide fellow UTD students with valuable information and resources about the dental field. Through general meetings, symposiums, guest speakers, dental school visits, and much more, our members can gain the knowledge they need to successfully apply to dental school. The Pre-Dental Association also takes part in campus-wide events that allow it’s members to raise funds for various community dental care funds and create awareness for the importance of oral health for all. All of these components come together to create a very unique opportunity for UTD students to be part of an organization that gives back to its members and to the community.

Please visit our website for more information! www.utdpda.com.

Title: Pre-Health Professionals Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Pre-Health Professionals Club shall serve as a student organization accessible to all students with interest in pursuing a health-related career. Through endeavors both on and off-campus, the students of the Pre-Health Professionals Club shall aim to lead the efforts to unite Pre-Health studies and resources of all shapes and forms, while making a positive impact on the greater campus community.

Title: Pre-Occupational Therapy & Pre-Physical Therapy Association UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission: To address the academic and pre-professional aspects of preparation for Pre-OT and Pre-PT students during their undergraduate training at UTD.

Our purpose is to facilitate a community of Occupational and Physical Therapy interested students. Overall, we are dedicated to preparing students for their future careers and for graduate school through volunteering, fundraising, application information, shadowing and tech job connections, guest speakers, and leadership opportunities.

Title: Pre-Optometry Professional Society
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: POPS was first introduced to UTD in 2006, and its purpose was simple. POPS aims to inform prospective optometry students about the field of optometry, provide guidance regarding admission requirements, and assist members in becoming competitive applicants for their prospective schools. POPS also provides access to optometry related events and job opportunities from local doctors, giving our members a chance to further develop their social and technical skills through first hand experience. Additionally, POPS aims to be a community leader by giving students an opportunity to give back through volunteering, whether optometry related or not. Other than providing guidance and opportunities, POPS gives students a chance to meet new friends and peers with similar interests, building a foundation for networking in the future.

Title: Pre-Pharmacy Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The UTD Pre-Pharmacy Association connects students interested in pharmacy with professionals in the field as well as pharmacy school representatives. We also share tips and tricks to passing the PCAT and PTCE exams and how to become a pharmacy technician. Come to our monthly meetings to learn about this evolving healthcare profession!

Title: Pre-Physician Assistant Society
Category: Academic Interest, Social, Educational/Departmental, Special Interest
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Pre-Physician Assistant Society (PPAS) is a student-led organization at UTD committed to preparing Pre-PA students towards their future career. By providing academic resources, encouraging volunteerism, and supplying healthcare/patient care experience opportunities, the PPAS readies students for the next step in their Physician Assistant endeavors.

Title: Preproduction Collaborative
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Preproduction Collaborative is a creativity-based club where we take the ideas of our members and help to develop them!

Title: Pride@UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Pride at UTD serves as a safe social forum for members of the community and allies  to meet and discuss thoughts, ideas, and issues concerning sexual orientation and gender identity.

Title: Process Automation and Reengineering Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Process Automation and Reengineering Club (PARC) aspires to elicit the interest of individuals in understanding Business Processes, Digital Transformation methodologies, Low Code, and related automation frameworks. We aim to equip the members with the best possible low code tools to identify the gaps in any process, mitigate the effects, and automate any Business Process in an Enterprise.

Title: Product Base
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: We are on a mission to spread awareness about Product Management to the UT Dallas students.

Title: Professional Asian Women-Student Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: At PAW(SA), our mission statement is to let all female Asian students shine in the professional world through fellowship, education, and empowerment. By collaborating with our sponsors, the Dallas Japanese Career Women (DJCW), we aim to connect working Asian women with students to provide social and learning experiences. Come join us for mentorship opportunities, networking, and community bonding!

Title: Project Management Club at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Project Management Club at UT-Dallas aims to promote and encourage Project Management related activities at The University of Texas at Dallas through networking, speaker events, workshops, mock exams, and reviews for professional certifications such as PMP ITIL. It's a common platform for students interested in Project Management and related fields.

Title: Public Policy & Political Economy Club UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: To provide an interactive platform for students in Public Policy and Political Economy (PPPE). We aim to organize events to bring together students, faculty members, alumni members and industry professionals to create networking opportunities, develop professional and analytical skills, and educate students regarding the prospects in the area of Public Policy and Political Economy. By encouraging students to step outside the classroom and network with fellow students, faculty and professionals, ‘The Club’ aims to build positive relationships between students and faculty at UT Dallas, and the Dallas/Fort Worth policy makers and political economist community.
President's Full Name: Andrés Ricardo Sánchez De la Rosa

Title: Quality Assurance Organization
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
'Test, Document, Improve'
Quality Assurance Organization is a student organization
focused on learning QA practices, providing detailed bug report,
and just having a good time breaking (digital) things. We hope
to improve our members as QA testers with hands on
experience, and provide students valuable feedback on their
projects at the same time. We are typically open to testing any
project a student would want testing, let it be a video game,
software, board game, etc.

Title: Quality Assurance Organization
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Quality Assurance Organization aims to aid UTD students by testing their projects for feedback and critique, as well as develop the skills of our members in the fields of Quality Assurance and User Experience testing. This organization hopes to reach every discipline at UTD, since QA testing isn't exclusive to one industry!

Title: Queer and Trans People of Color at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission: To Promote the Herstory, Academic Success, Health, Civic Engagement, and Empowerment of all queer and trans people of color
Purpose/Description: To provide a safe social environment to express the varying intersectional identities that come with being a queer and/or trans person of color. We strive to promote the History, Academic Success, Health, Civic Engagement, and Empowerment of all Queer and Trans People Of Color as well as to create a space for chosen families to foster specfically for queer and/or trans students of color who may not be fully embraced in all of their identities.

Title: Radio UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: As the student-run radio station for the University of Texas at Dallas, Radio UTD provides both music and talk show entertainment to the campus community. Founded in 2002, Radio UTD has grown steadily thanks to help from the University, student body, and ever supportive Dallas music scene. Our music blog is comprised of student bloggers who write album reviews, feature articles, and even cover local concerts. We also have a project in collaboration with UTDTV called Pseudo Stereo, in which we record sessions with local and touring bands on the UTD campus.

Title: Rainbow Six Siege Club at UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Rainbow Six Siege Club at UTD’s purpose is to foster positive relationships among its members through playing Tom Clancy’s Rainbow Six Siege casually and competitively. The club also should create an environment conducive to competitive success and allow for the creation of a healthy competitive environment.  Falling under the Club Sports and Recreation categories, we make it our priority for our members to have an experience that increases their interest, knowledge, and skill in the game, as well as having a group they can go to in order to relax, unwind, or grind with after a long day in classes.

Title: Rangam Indian Classical Dance Team
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Rangam is the premiere competitive Indian classical dance team at the University of Texas at Dallas. We hope to bring awareness to the various different forms of Indian classical dance and share the art form with the UT Dallas community. Our dance team will give our members a means to channel their individuality, create bonds with one another, and continue to practice a form of dance that they have learned for many years. Our mission is to promote unity and discipline. Students will learn to make the most of the constructive criticism they receive during practice. This not only helps build their character but bolsters their perseverance. We will always approach our teammates with respect and dignity. As Rangam competes nationally and performs around the DFW metroplex, the University of Texas at Dallas will not only gain local recognition but be nationally known. Advertising this classical team will not only attract prospective students but will also showcase the diversity and talent at UT Dallas.

Title: Reaching and Inspiring Student Engineers
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: To create and present a STEM-focused curriculum to K-12 students through this service organization, inspiring them to pursue a degree/major in an engineering-related field.

Title: Real Estate Club at UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Inform. Connect. Develop

The Real Estate Club at the Herbert D. Weitzman Institute for Real Estate at the University of Texas at Dallas is committed to:
 
- Providing informative insights into the various real estate career fields available to students.
- Connecting students to meaningful internship and job opportunities.
- The medium to develop professional relationships with like-minded students and professionals passionate about real estate.

Title: Reasonable Faith
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Reasonable Faith at UTD is a Christian apologetics organization that discusses how Christianity could be reasonable using Science, History, and Philosophy.

Title: Reformed University Fellowship International
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Reformed University Fellowship International (RUF-I) seeks to welcome and show hospitality to students and scholars from all nations; explore the gospel of Jesus Christ with interested students and scholars; and equip those students and scholars who want to honor and enjoy God in every area of life.

Title: Reptile Club of UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: We are a passionate group of individuals with a love of reptiles, amphibians, and other exotics alike. Our goal is to provide a social interest group, a place for students to get information about exotic animals, and help spread awareness of wildlife conservation, rescue, and care. As an organization our donations and funds will contribute to the organization's events, materials needed, and be donated to Nature's Edge Wildlife and Reptile Rescue in Fort Worth Texas. Making us a mix of social, service, and education as an organization. If you love reptiles/wildlife, or are just curious, come check us out!

Title: Riichi Mahjong Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Mahjong is a traditional Asian game that is unique from all of the other tabletop games. The Riichi Mahjong Club values sportsmanship and the spirit of competition. We encourage important skills like observation, risk management, and strategy. Since there are many international students in UTD and mahjong is famous in Asia, we hope this mahjong club can help American students to touch the Asian culture, and help those international students to find their fellows.

Title: RoboSub
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The mission of RoboSub is to inspire and challenge students to learn and creatively apply science, technology, engineering, and math to solve real-world problems. We strengthen our member's critical thinking, collaboration, entrepreneurship, and innovation skills. Our purpose is to build a robotic submarine to compete in inter-collegiate competitions.

Title: Rocket League Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: To promote and foster a community around Rocket League at UTD

Title: Roosevelt at UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The Roosevelt Network has a membership of 1,000 students across the United States working to research and advance solutions in their communities to pressing policy problems that affect them every day. Members are organized at over 130 campuses in 40 states nationwide to work with policymakers and communicators to write and enact local, state, and national policy.

Focusing on seven broad issue areas – education, the economy, human rights, democratic access, energy, and the environment, foreign policy, and healthcare – campus branches of the Roosevelt Network provide opportunities for the next generation of leaders to advocate for their communities. At UT Dallas, we work to understand the issues facing our campus, city, and the greater Texas population through in-depth, data-driven research projects and work with local leaders to implement policy change.

Title: Roundnet Club, UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: We seek to foster a community of diverse individuals who enjoy the sport of roundnet(also known as spikeball). We desire to be a club that is both recreational and competitive catering to both the competitive and casual player.

Title: SAP User's Group
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
About  -
The SAP Users’ Group (SUG) at UTD was established in August 2004 and has grown quickly ever since. SUG prepares students for careers in accounting, sales, marketing, business operations, computer information technology, and other professions. SUG provides hands-on experience with the SAP software package, helping students get a head start in their careers.
Mission - 
The mission of SUG is to help students establish a future career in SAP or other enterprise E-Business platforms as a systems user, in IT systems support, in consulting, systems evaluation, or other career opportunities that become available as enterprise systems and SAP evolve.
 Purpose -
Offers students opportunities to network with professionals, faculty and other students interested in SAP and Enterprise Systems.
• Educates students through Enterprise Systems and SAP related courses available at both the undergraduate and 
  graduate levels.
• Includes students in the SAP Users Group resume book and helps many students find internships and full-time 
 jobs.
• Works in partnership with the Center for Information Technology and Management to give students projects 
  and hands on experience.
• Offers SAP information sessions presented from local companies that use SAP.
• Provide access to SAP educational materials, workshops, conferences, SAP Contests for students, job portals and 
  SAP professionals.

Title: Scarcity Project
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The purpose of this organization is to allow students the opportunity to directly collaborate with organizations that are dedicated to providing sufficient sources of food, water, shelter, clothing and education around the world. This organization group falls under service as we plan on promoting education among peers and populations all over the world that are in need of services of a various array of services. Through this organization we plan on targeting a population source that is directly in need of aid in the realm of reading and writing (education), food, shelter and clothing. The service of the organization is not limited to the United States, but plans on doing work on a global platform. With the growing trend of this organization we hope to provide UTD students with ample opportunity to participate in volunteer services, mentorship, tutoring programs as well as several different fundraising projects that will directly raise funds to donate to different foundations. We believe that everyone should have an equal shot at life and providing adequate supplies to sustain life would be a huge step towards this cause. Through establishing this organization the aim will be to coordinate and work with differing projects and connect UTD students to these organizations devoted to promoting global relief. We value dedication, teamwork, empathy and equal opportunity for everyone so that they can receive the education that they deserve.

Title: Scientific Computing Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Many incoming freshmen, sophomores, and even long-standing upperclassmen will major in a STEM subject, none the wiser to the importance of having command of one or more programming languages for scientific use. With the increasing availability of PCs, and the outstanding need to perform more rigorous and expensive calculations, knowing how to code a simple algorithm makes a proper difference in the eligibility of a job applicant. Knowing this, the Scientific Computing Club intends to (with a multi-disciplinary approach) inform interested students about methods in scientific computing, and to provide the experience of working in tandem with a computer to expand the range of a future scientist or engineer to tackle problems that are impossible with the range of pen and paper.

Modern problems in STEM will be solved for with the use of computers to illustrate the need for knowledge of these methods, and opportunities that may make use of the knowledge learned from this club (employment, research, etc.) will be provided to interested students. Students will be able to tie these methods to current courses to gain intimate experience about the benefits of computing literacy. The rudimentary goal of the Scientific Computing Club is to allow students to confidently work concurrently with computing methods to grow their confidence in future pursuits, and to become a better scientist overall.

This organization will fall under the “Academic Interest” category for groups in the SOC.

Title: Sculpture Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Sculpture Club aims to provide a supportive and encouraging community art environment. Students will be able to seek feedback and collaborate on projects. 
Sculpture club values encouragement, collaboration, creativity, exploration of mediums, and community.

Title: Service-dog In Training (SIT)
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: UTD SIT is a collegiate group supported by Canine Companions. We give students the opportunity to raise and train puppies with Canine Companions, and these puppies go on to do great things. These puppies may go on to be service dogs for those with physical or mental disabilities, hearing aids, facility dogs, or as various other supporters.

Title: Service-dogs In Training (SIT)
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The mission of UTD Service-dogs In Training (SIT), a chapter of Collegiate Canine Companions for Independence (C3I), is to connect the UT Dallas student, staff, and faculty to the community through volunteer service with Canine Companions for Independence.  Assistance dogs make a life changing difference in the community, and we want to make UT Dallas a part of that change.  The UT Dallas campus and its people can be the support needed to allow these puppies to grow into the trained professionals required by Canine Companions for Independence.  By puppy raising, puppy sitting, or volunteer and outreach we can affect a positive change in the lives of people in our local community, Texas, and beyond.

The purpose of UTD Service-dogs In Training (SIT), a chapter of Collegiate Canine Companions for Independence (C3I), is to help Canine Companions for Independence in caring for puppies. Canine Companions recently opened a training campus in Irving, Texas. They breed and train assistance dogs that they give to people with disabilities at no cost to the recipient. Our job as Puppy Raisers or Puppy Sitters is to provide a place to live as they grow up, to socialize them to other dogs and humans, and to help in the training process. It takes some dedication, but it is a rewarding part of the process.  UTD SIT will also have general volunteers to assist Canine Companions for Independence with their need for volunteers at fundraisers, awareness and outreach, and various other tasks asked of their normal volunteer.

Title: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Through friendship, scholarship, leadership, and service, SAE finds men with impeccable character and helps them achieve their full potential, letting them rise up to face every challenge placed before them—today and tomorrow. We achieve this by grooming our members to become True Gentlemen.

Finally, the sorority shall maintain respect for the views of others through this valuing, thereby enhancing our understanding of one another, and thus bettering our community, our country, and the world.

Title: Skate Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Mission/Purpose:  To introduce new skaters to skating and to allow skaters to spend time with others who share the same interest. We would like to foster an environment that motivates others to skate.

As an affiliated student chapter, SHRM at UT Dallas has access to a national network of student chapters, assistance, billing autonomy, are distinguished as being apart of the world's largest HR Association, provided chapter supplies, and experience the "real world" of HR, while participating in the student chapter merit award program, leadership development opportunities, and networking opportunities.

Title: Student Government

The mission of the Student Government is to ensure that UT Dallas is fully committed to serving the collective interests of the student body. In this capacity, we serve as the official student voice to UT Dallas administration, the UT System Board of Regents, and other governmental entities.





The Student Government seeks to enhance spirit, protect University traditions, and support all students and student organizations in their academic and community-directed endeavors.





For further information, including information about elections, meeting times, and past projects, please visit our website at sg.utdallas.edu.

Title: Student Organization Center
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The Student Organization Center, affectionately known as SOC, assists students in connecting to the UT Dallas community. There are over 300 registered student organizations on campus. Wherever your passions lie, you're sure to find a place to call home.

We encourage all Comets to Explore. Engage. Create.

Title: Student Tutors Reviving Identity through Volunteerism and Education
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
STRIVE was founded in 2019 to help underprivileged students in the DFW metroplex have the chance to live up to their true potential. Our initiative first started as a means for Refugee and ESL students to work with select mentors. Our work focused on helping the students overcome language barriers, learn that diversity is indeed accepted, and to think beyond primary education. This initiative was a success as we had a lot of students interested in higher education after various discussions.

Unfortunately, many of them struggled to gain acceptance due to the compounding effects of the language barrier. Without a solid grasp of English, performance in various other subjects takes a significant hit. Based on this dilemma, we decided that it was essential to develop a tutoring program for these students to connect them with tutor-mentors from similar backgrounds who would guide the students and allow them to overcome this barrier.

We see a bright future for these students, and we want to tap into their potential by providing them with one-on-one subject strategy training and educational advice. Our motto is that every student should have the right to succeed regardless of their background. Language should not be an obstacle to success. We currently work with Lake Highlands High School in the Richardson Independent School District (RISD).  Currently looking to expand, we look forward to continuing our mission to eliminate educational disparities.

Title: Student Veterans Association
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The UT Dallas Student Veterans Association is a chapter of the Student Veterans of America (SVA). The student organization is sponsored by the Military and Veteran Center (MVC) and focuses on professional development, community service, and social functions.

Please contact the SVA officers at this email: UTDvets@utdallas.edu. Go to this link for more information: https://utdallas.edu/veterans/org/.

Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our club aims to educate UTD students in an effort to reverse stereotypes and misconceptions about the Middle East while raising funds for emergency relief. Our organization will host informational meetings, culture nights, and fundraisers among many other events.

Title: Students Promoting Acts of Random Kindness (SPARK)
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Students Promoting Acts of Random Kindness, or SPARK, aims to bring kindness to UTDallas both on and off-campus through volunteering events. SPARK requires no membership dues and no minimum attendance rules. We only require good vibes and a positive spirit!

Title: Tabletop RPG Club at the University of Texas at Dallas

Title: Taking Real Understanding To Heart Bible club (T.R.U.T.H Bible club)
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Reveal the profound mystery of Elohim God, God the Mother, and other prophecies testified in the bible to UTD students, volunteer, and do other events and activities.

Title: TaRaas

Title: Tau Beta Pi
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Organization Description:
Tau Beta Pi is universally recognized as the preeminent engineering honor society that students of all engineering disciplines strive to join. Founded in 1885 at Lehigh University, Tau Beta Pi is the oldest engineering honor society in the United States. There are now chapters in 250 US colleges and universities, 47 active alumni chapters in 16 districts across the country, and a total initiated membership of approximately 607,000.

The Texas Xi chapter was officially registered on February 15, 2020. Our chapter strives to promote future growth by nurturing integrity, character and service. As a new chapter, our current objective is to increase awareness of the chapter on campus, through professional, service and recreational events.

Mission and Purpose:
As the only academic honor society that confers recognition on individuals from all engineering disciplines, Tau Beta Pi:
- Recognizes those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Maters by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character -- as students in engineering or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering
- Provides leadership and professional development training for engineering students and alumni members.
- Promotes lifelong alumnus member involvement and cultivates a community of dedicated high achievers

Title: Teal Comets
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our goal is to prevent and increase awareness about sexual assault and misconduct through our five objectives: activism, fundraising, education, solidarity, and volunteerism.

Title: TechPact

We aim to work towards building a positive impact on society and maintaining long-term relationships between the local ventures and student fraternity for mutual benefit.

Purpose-

Title: TEDxUTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
TEDxUTD is a student organization at The University of Texas at Dallas. TEDx was created in the spirit of TED's mission, "ideas worth spreading," supporting independent organizers who want to create a TED-like event in their own community. TEDxUTD expands on this mission, bringing ideas worth spreading to UT Dallas.

We are a student-run, faculty-sponsored, organization at the University of Texas at Dallas. We have successfully held conferences in the past and look forward to holding more TEDx events in the future to inspire innovation on our campus.

Title: Texas Rising UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Mission:
We are a movement of diverse, young Texans working to leverage power through leadership development, issue advocacy, community organizing and elections.

Purpose:
Texas Rising – a project of the Texas Freedom Network – is building the power of young people in our communities and at the ballot box. Our program organizes and builds power with young people of color, in a multi-issue, intersectional social justice framework. 

We focus on:
- Voter registration and turnout of young Texans
- Organizing on college campuses
- Youth leadership development and trainings
- Grassroots and digital organizing
- Issue advocacy

Organization Description: 

Texas Rising is a student-led political organization dedicated to empowering young voters and promoting progressive ideals on campus and in our community through voter registration and issue advocacy.

Title: The Drawing Sessions
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Drawing Sessions is a multi-disciplinary fine arts club whose purpose is to celebrate drawing as a shared interest. Title: The Historical Swordfighting Guild of UT Dallas

Title: The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers at the University of Texas at Dallas

Title: The Lavender Club

This club intends to foster a safe and welcoming environment for those who feel ostracized and encourage those to facilitate their voices. Finally, we strive to create close relationships and build a community for young queer women and nonbinary students in the DFW area.

Title: The Mercury

If you are interested in being hired as a writer/reporter, graphics artist, or photographer, please email our editor-in-chief at editor@utdmercury.com to learn more about the application process. If you would like to write an op-ed, see our website for more information. 

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions, and don't hesitate to check us out on social media or on our website.

Title: The National Society of Leadership and Success (NSLS)

Title: The Product League

To achieve this, we offer opportunities for learning and skill-building through events like case study competitions and book club meetings. We also provide networking opportunities, allowing students to connect with others who share their interests and potentially find internships or job opportunities. Our organization aims to build a strong community of individuals interested in product management, which can foster collaboration and innovation. Finally, we strive to enhance UT Dallas's reputation by offering high-quality events and producing successful case studies. Overall, our organization is committed to empowering Product Management enthusiasts and driving innovation in the field of product development.

Title: The Taekwondo Club at The University of Texas at Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Taekwondo Club at The University of Texas at Dallas is a recreational group that aims to teach Taekwondo to students of all skill levels who are interested in the sport. Providing a space for engaging in this fulfilling and social hobby is our main goal, but we welcome all UTD students with even the most passing interest in learning about other languages and cultures.

Title: Treasure Ministries

Title: University Blood Initiative - UT Dallas Chapter

Title: User Experience (UX) Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: User Experience (UX) is the design process of making products and services that are easy to use and delightful to end-users. UX focuses on understanding user goals, needs, and behaviors by using empathy and design thinking. As the UX Club, we aim to learn UX theory and practice through lectures, workshops, and conferences. We support each other as a community by encouraging sharing and collaboration networks, developing positive relationships with industry professionals and alumni, and promoting awareness and understanding of UX through mentoring and volunteering.

Title: User Experience Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Since 2014, The User Experience Club began its journey to spread knowledge and awareness of the UX industry at UT Dallas. We noticed the absence of a prominent UX program on campus, and wanted to share the fruitful prospects of this niche career field. Even through the pandemic, with excellent leadership and keen understanding of our club members’ needs, our presence has been ever growing. Our club continues to bring more resources, pathways, and opportunities to our members each year, through various events such as our annual Design Conference, industry networking events, UX workshops, and much more.

Title: UTD Terry Scholars
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The purpose of UTDTS is to uphold and exemplify the ideals for which the Terry Foundation stands: scholarship, leadership, service and community.

 

Mission: By providing a humble example of strong leadership, consistent academic excellence, and dedicated service to all, the UTDTS strives to better the university, community, and ourselves.

 

Organization: Membership is comprised of those scholars selected by the Terry Foundation and who are in good standing. The UTDallas Terry Scholars organization is affiliated with the Hobson Wildenthal Honors College, is overseen by 6-10 officers elected each spring, and holds bi-weekly meetings for all members.

Title: UTD TV
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Welcome to UTD TV! (Click me)

UTD TV aims to create high quality content to inform and entertain the UT Dallas community while providing production experience for our members.

We typically have several YouTube series running at a time spanning a variety of topics from unsolved mysteries, gaming, to on-campus activities. The active shows depend on what has been pitched by our members for the semester. At the same time we normally have creative production underway, working on a larger short film per semester. This short film goes through all stages of production from script revision to editing and distribution, allowing TV members to gain production experience all across the board.

Our scheduled shows and short films are submitted several times a year for competition at national college festivals like CBI or AFF. In the past we've won awards for best station promo, sports show, and more!


If you have any questions at all don't hesitate to reach out to stationmanager@utdtv.com, or Fults, Payton Baylie on Microsoft Teams.

Title: UTDesign Makerspace
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The mission of UTDesign Makerspace is to provide an environment of innovation, collaboration, and education where Makers can develop their skills and projects among their peers with access to current and next-generation technology.

Title: UTDuelists
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: To provide students on campus a local place to explore their interests and hobbies and learn about the card game, “Yu-Gi-Oh!” with other students in a friendly manner without needing to buy cards or drive to card shops in both the casual and competitive styles of the game.

Title: Vaalmeengal Tamil Sangam
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
The mission of UTD Vaalmeengal Tamil Sangam is to provide a mutually supportive and positive learning
the environment in which every individual member can develop communication and leadership skills.
In order to carry out this purpose, this club shall:
• Uphold and treasure the Tamil art, culture, and heritage.
• Celebrate Tamil festivals and functions.
• Provide for its individual members' fair and constructive evaluation of their efforts
toward self-improvement.
• Serve as one platform for all Tamil-speaking people to unite and delight the identity
amongst multiple cultures.
• Edify our youth with the richness, knowledge, and history of the Tamil language and culture.
• Help its individual members improve their abilities to communicate effectively and
impart leadership training for all its individual members

Title: Vietnamese International Network of Culture, Education, and Friendship
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Our mission & purpse: to connect Vietnamese international students at UTD with each other and to contribute to the University's diverse student base.
Organization description: Our organization was created to serve as a place for Vietnamese international students & immigrants to gather & meet those of a similar background. We primarily host socials and games for our members, as well as participate in cultural activities like performing Vietnamese dances or holding cultural workshops.

Title: Vietnamese Student Association at The University of Texas at Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The mission of VSA-UTD shall be to promote unity among multicultural backgrounds at the University of Texas at Dallas. The purpose of VSA-UTD shall be to create a safe environment in which a strong, cohesive network of friends and family can be built while participating in university functions, taking part in philanthropic activities, and promoting awareness and appreciation of Vietnamese culture. We will strive to enhance the welfare of the Vietnamese American community and will work diligently to preserve and cultivate Vietnamese American culture.

Title: Volunteer Proofreaders for Project Gutenberg
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Volunteer Proofreaders for Project Gutenberg was formed to provide a completely virtual volunteer opportunity and bring awareness to the vast availability of open access public domain ebooks and online sources. We work together with the 501(c)3 nonprofit ‘Project Gutenberg’ and their affiliate ‘Distributed Proofreaders’. Our organization values philanthropy, access to free information, preserving history, and increasing the knowledge and resources available for our current and future generations.

As a volunteer, you will be given a side-by-side screen of a scanned page from a copyright-free book and a program-generated text that should match the scan. Your job is simply to check for any mistakes that were made in the program’s scan, while also following certain guidelines for formatting the text. These corrections are simple and easy to do, and you can work from your personal laptop at any time. No prior experience is required, and there are no minimum or maximum hours required for membership.

To get started, click on the forms tab, watch our asynchronous orientation video, and fill out the accompanying form.

If you have further questions, please reach out to Manish Samson, manish.samson@utdallas.edu.

Title: Wesley at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
To promote a campus Christian movement and concerned Christian ministry of the educational community; to witness in the campus community to the mission, message, and life of Jesus Christ; to deepen, enrich, and mature the Christian faith of college and university students, faculty, and staff through commitment to Jesus Christ and the Church and to assist them in their service and leadership to world, in and through the Church.

The vision is to provide a safe space for college students to discover and live out their faith as disciples of Jesus Christ with a commitment to progressive biblical theology, inclusiveness, missions, and community.

Title: Wind Energy Club
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The Wind Energy Club is dedicated to promoting knowledge and engagement in the field of wind energy on campus. We want our students to be fully immersed in wind energy and the power it holds for our future. Our goal is to connect students with renewable energy opportunities on campus, including participation in the Collegiate Wind Competition and on-campus research opportunities.

Title: WinSTEM
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Win STEM at UT Dallas is a charitable, service-oriented organization aimed at supporting and recruiting ambitious women to pursue STEM careers through building confidence, community engagement, and opportunity guidance.

Title: Wishmakers
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our mission is to raise money to donate to the Make-a-Wish foundation to aid children who are ill. We organize and execute events where we fundraise and engage students on campus to spread awareness of the organization.

Title: Women in Animation at UT Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The purpose of WIA is to bring together a community of current and future animation professionals to empower and support women, gender queer, and non-binary identifying students in the art, science, and business of animation. WIA's goal is to promote diversity in leadership roles for underrepresented communities and reach 50/50 representation by 2025 in the animation and games industries.

Title: Women in Cybersecurity
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Our chapter encourages women to pursue careers in the highly dynamic and expansive field of cybersecurity by offering tools, events, and networking opportunities throughout the year.

The purposes and goals of WiCyS are:
1. To support, engage, and encourage women pursuing careers in fields of cybersecurity.
2. To provide opportunities for personal and professional development while cultivating a community of individuals with similar goals and interests.

Title: Women in Neuroscience
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Our purpose is to provide resources, mentorship, and friendship to our members, with the goal of empowering women in the field of neuroscience. We aim to create a network where women who are completing their PhD in Neuroscience are supported in their personal experiences by peers navigating similar experiences.

Title: Women in Technology & Business
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Women in Technology and Business is a professional organization to support and empower women in technology and business fields and provide them with resources to be successful. In addition, WITB aims to break down the stereotypes surrounding technology and business fields, expose more people to how technology can impact their career path, and encourage more women to join technology and business fields.

Title: Women Mentoring Women In Engineering
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: The purpose of Women Mentoring Women in Engineering is to increase retention of women in the engineering workplace through providing access to long-term and recurring mentoring relationships with women in industry or with graduate students on their respective college campuses.

Title: Women Pursuing Law
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Women Pursuing Law's (WPL) goal is to provide resources specifically geared towards empowering women in law, politics, and social justice. In an industry where women face unique obstacles such as gender inequality, WPL will be a source of information, education, and inspiration to help future women and non-binary conforming leaders enter the field with confidence.

Title: Women Who Compute
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Our mission is to promote, celebrate, and support women in technology fields by providing
events with professional and personal development opportunities.

Title: Writing Without Purpose
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: Writing Without Purpose is a creative writing club in which members participate in weekly writing prompts and showcase them. The type of writing varies from week to week and the prompts are short and simple to allow the writers as much creative liberty as possible. The purpose of our club is to provide people with a love for any form of writing with an outlet to express their ideas and creativity in a safe, judgment-free space. It is our goal to create an environment where one can facilitate their own creative identity, polish their skills, develop or discover new talents, and enjoy the works of others while being able to bounce ideas off of each other.

Title: XR UTD
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr: XR UTD aims to provide the UTD community with a social and educational forum to learn about, enjoy, and contribute to the understanding and utilization of virtual and augmented reality technologies.

Title: Young Democratic Socialists of America at The University of Texas at Dallas
Mission, Purpose, and Organization Descr:
Our Mission is to carry out political education and help out community on and around campus, we believe in a better society being possible where people are truly in control of their works and lives, and believe power should not be in the hands of a few. YDSA works to enact change through activities, education, agitation, and work with other organizations to help advance these goals and promote a better future.

The working class, marginalized people are core to change and helping. Climate change, imperialism, sexism, civil rights, and other issues are abound and we aim to help on these fronts.

A better world is possible, so lets get started today.
